content:
Context: 1. INTRODUTION AND DEFINATION > 1.1 Introdution
Content: [1.1 Introdution]
This Disaster Recovery Derective describes the strategy and procedures for recovering
Data Center processing of applications should a disaster substantially disrupt
operations.
The plan is organized into two parts: the main body provides a general description of
the disaster recovery strategy and program; the appendices provide detailed information
for conducting the recovery. The main body is public information and may be freely
distributed; the appendices and attachments contain sensitive information that is
restricted to the individuals responsible for recovering Data Center operations.
The plan is frequently updated to reflect current hardware, software, procedures,
applications, and staffing. Revisions are distributed to the disaster recovery team
members perform twice a year following the disaster recovery tests.

Context: 1. INTRODUTION AND DEFINATION > 1.2 Terms and Definitions > 1.2.1 Definition of Disaster Avoidance/Recovery
Content: [1.2.1 Definition of Disaster Avoidance/Recovery]
Figure 1 gives an overview of the used definitions of Disaster recovery describing
avoidance and recovery measures in the field of Business respectively IT. As the puzzle
pieces in the figure symbolize, business and IT have to work together, have to join their
forces to succeed in continuity planning.
While IT is responsible for disaster avoidance, disaster recovery and support of
business in re-establishing data completeness, business has the responsibility of the
data entry and the business continuity processes. Therefore, responsible business
managers of Group companies have to elaborate a continuity plan, have to invoke this
plan in case of disaster and have the lead in re-establishing data completeness.
Figure 1: Business / IT continuity, avoidance / recovery
Definition of RPO/RTO Objective of transactional system
To quantify the performance in the field of disaster avoidance and recovery the following
definitions are used (see Figure 2 and Table 1):
Figure 2: RPO/RTO objective for SAP/ SUN application
Abbreviation Description Target of PHV
RPO Recovery Point Objective: Point in time to where Max. 30 Minutes
consistent application data exists
RTO (Recovery Time Objective): Time to get systems Max. 4 – 8 hours
running with data of the last recovery point
DCTO (Data Convergence Time Objective): Time to Max. 8 – 24 hours
revert to a fully restored system (all data
available)

Context: 1. INTRODUTION AND DEFINATION > 1.2 Terms and Definitions > 1.2.2 Definition of Cold Site for RSC
Content: [1.2.2 Definition of Cold Site for RSC]
PHV approved as permanance measure that for RSC office has to establish 1 cold
site at Truong Dinh Store. A cold site is defined as a disaster recovery solution that
provides office space and redundant network connections. All equipment is
preparation with limited of function to continue operations has to be installed during
the disaster.

Context: 1. INTRODUTION AND DEFINATION > 1.2 Terms and Definitions > 1.2.3 Definition of Disaster and Relevant Scenarios
Content: [1.2.3 Definition of Disaster and Relevant Scenarios]
PHV defines disaster concerning on the PHV application at RSC which is
centralization for all PHV business application as follows five scenarios as driver for
requirements and basis for defining actions (see Figure 3).
A: Networking - Issues with MPLS or leased line connections, internet
connection
B: Server host VMware - Problems with two ESXi servers not functioning properly.
C: Data Center - Inability to access the office data center.
D: Core Switch - An error related to the core network switch.
E: Application - An application error preventing it from starting.
Figure 3: Identified five scenarios

Context: 1. INTRODUTION AND DEFINATION > 1.2 Terms and Definitions > 1.2.4 Definition of the Crisis Phases
Content: [1.2.4 Definition of the Crisis Phases]
PHV defines the following three Crisis Phases (see Figure 4).
Normal Incident Management (normal situation) - Level 1
Normal incident management which is covered by SLA corresponds with the daily
business of PHV. Normal operational issues without any business critical impact can
occur but they can be handled within the business continuity management of the
affected ITD. No coordinating Crisis Team has to be invoked. After a short interruption,
business can work normally.
Emergency – Level 2
An incident has occurred with impact on one or several store, one application or several
applications. The Crisis Team is invoked and can handle the incident/emergency
according to the predefined proceeding below the actual scenario. The disaster
recovery planning is under control.
ITD is under the Emergency team. Excluding IT manager is team member of Crisis
team
Crisis - Level 3
The impact of the incident has reached a level where employees or PHV as company
are seriously threatened. The whole companies are affected, the services of PHV can
only be provided with considerable constraints. The Crisis Team (level 3) is in action.
For business critical application, emergency phase starts with interruptions > 8h
The Crisis team is define in detail in the PHV BCP Manual guide
Figure 4: Crisis phases (administrative applications mean: mail, office, etc…)

metadata.hierarchy: 1. INTRODUTION AND DEFINATION > 1.2 Terms and Definitions > 1.2.4 Definition of the Crisis Phases

content:
Context: 1. INTRODUTION AND DEFINATION > 1.2 Terms and Definitions > 1.2.5 Definition of Crisis Level and Crisis Teams
Content: [1.2.5 Definition of Crisis Level and Crisis Teams]
PHV defines 3 different Crisis Levels (1, 2, 3) with corresponding Crisis Teams (see
Chapter 2.4):
• Crisis level 1 is active in normal incident management
• Crisis level 2 is active in the crisis phase “Emergency”
• Crisis level 3 is active in the crisis phase “Crisis” and following of the PHV
BCP Manual guide

Criteria for activating crisis level 1:
• Normal incident management which is covered by SLA corresponds with the
daily business of PHV. Normal operational issues without any business
critical impact can occur but they can be handled within the business
continuity management of the affected ITD. No coordinating Crisis Team has
to be invoked. After a short interruption, business can work normally
Criteria for activating crisis level 2:
• Interruption of business critical applications > time agreed on SLA
• One or several store, one application or several application are affected by
the problem
• Problem solving is complex because of the involvement of various teams
fix structure of the organization of crisis level 2 can shorten recovery time
Criteria for activating crisis level 3:
• Interruption of business critical applications > 8h or SLA cannot be fulfilled
anymore
• Problem has huge impact on business therefore structure with
permanent members of IT and business is needed to make right decisions
quickly
• Problem has big management attention good communication and
reporting structures are needed

content:
Context: 3. CRISIS SCENARIO > 3.1 Office cannot accessible - Loss of Data Center / Vital Infrastructure
Content: [3.1 Office cannot accessible - Loss of Data Center / Vital Infrastructure]
The loss of data center or its vital infrastructure is characterized by any of the following
misbehaviors within the end-to-end process including IT:
• Many users are no longer able to use the IT environment and the business
critical applications provided by the ITSC (out of service)
• Many business critical processes and transactions performed by IT-systems
are stopped and probably lost
• The entire networking and communication infrastructure of the ITSC or of the
external suppliers fails
• The computing environment is no longer available
• Storage of data is no longer secured
• The ITSC local data backup and restore environment is no longer usable
Above-mentioned loss of data center might be caused by:
• External disaster like fire, thunderstorm, earthquake, flood or other
hazardous incidents
• Loss of power (electricity) (provider failure together with UPS running out of
power)

[DRP Technical Table]:
No | Function | Crisis Level | Role
1 | IT Manager | Level 2 | 2nd Approve resume service of production site
2 | IT Assistant Manager | Level 2 | 2nd Approve resume service of production site in the absence of IT Manager
3 | IT Officers | Lead of Level1 | 1st level support leader team - IT related error analysis - Coordination Power Users - Assistance IT Manager - Data backup monitoring - Coordination of maintenance tasks - Problem indication towards IT Manager (regional) or supplier
4 | IT Assistant Team | 1st level support - Problem Triage - Coordination software program issues - Specifies requests / coordinates hotel users - Executes tests - Helps Stores/RSC users in basic software program handling - Error analysis data output - Structures problem indication / documentation - Announces problems according to checklist - Manages system master data, e.g. create/delete POS articles - Responsible for replacement of consumables supplies - Responsible for PC/Screen cleaning

• Major air conditioning failure
• Human misbehavior (mistakes on changes, sabotage) including
unauthorized access to the data center
• Risks originated by external providers or partners (e.g. HW/SW vendor,
outsourcing company)
• Organizational risks
In all cases, the consequences will be the interruption of the access to the
business critical applications, resulting in the discontinuation of business
processes and their transactions in the systems.

metadata.hierarchy: 3. CRISIS SCENARIO > 3.1 Office cannot accessible - Loss of Data Center / Vital Infrastructure

content:
Context: 3. CRISIS SCENARIO > 3.1 Office cannot accessible - Loss of Data Center / Vital Infrastructure > 3.1.1 Most Important Avoidance Measures
Content: [3.1.1 Most Important Avoidance Measures]
The generic measures and possibilities to avoid any of the above-mentioned incidents
concerning loss of data center or part of its vital infrastructure are:
• Build data center infrastructure and its operation based on good practice. In
future, Holcim data center standards will be elaborated.
• Permanently audit and monitor the data center infrastructure in terms of
good practice
• Have all good practice measures for avoidance implemented
• Preventive maintenance of key equipment in the data center

metadata.hierarchy: 3. CRISIS SCENARIO > 3.1 Office cannot accessible - Loss of Data Center / Vital Infrastructure > 3.1.1 Most Important Avoidance Measures

content:
Context: 3. CRISIS SCENARIO > 3.1 Office cannot accessible - Loss of Data Center / Vital Infrastructure > 3.1.2 Most Important Recovery Measures
Content: [3.1.2 Most Important Recovery Measures]
• Establish a frame agreement with a backup data center provider, to switch
over to a second IT environment, based on backup data within DCTO time
frame (local cold-site, short-term measure)
• Establish a global frame agreement with a backup data center provider, to
switch over to a second IT environment, based on backup data within DCTO
time frame (local cold-site, global Holcim contract, mid-term measure)

Context: 3. CRISIS SCENARIO > 3.2 Datacenter MPLS link/ Leased Line Down - Outage of WAN/ Leased line
Content: [3.2 Datacenter MPLS link/ Leased Line Down - Outage of WAN/ Leased line]
Outage of WAN is characterized by any of the following misbehaviors within the end-
toend connectivity or communication of any user of the PHV company hub to the
ITSC:
• Loss of connectivity or interruption of one, more or all data links from the
shared service center to online system (Office 365, Azure..)
• Technology failure, data link problem or general trouble within the network of
the data link (WAN) provider
• Failover of intended redundancy technology not working
• Interruption by mis-configuration of malicious change of the environment
• Problems on the various interfaces between the components of the whole
end to end connection of the WAN
• Physical interruption of cables within buildings and/or outside on private and
public places
• Logical interruption of connection within data link components like firewall,
intrusion detection systems or switches and routers
All above-mentioned interruptions might be mainly caused by:
• External disaster like fire, thunderstorm, earthquake, flood and other
hazardous incidents
• Human misbehavior (mistakes on changes, sabotage) including
unauthorized access to network components
• Risks originated by the WAN provider or partner
• Organizational risks
• Data center risks including loss of power and failure in air conditioning
In all cases, the consequence will be the interruption of the data access to the
business critical applications, due to failure in the connectivity between PHV RSC
and Store also online system resulting in the discontinuation of business processes
and their transactions in the systems.

metadata.hierarchy: 3. CRISIS SCENARIO > 3.2 Datacenter MPLS link/ Leased Line Down - Outage of WAN/ Leased line

content:
Context: 3. CRISIS SCENARIO > 3.2 Datacenter MPLS link/ Leased Line Down - Outage of WAN/ Leased line > 3.2.1 Most Important Avoidance Measures
Content: [3.2.1 Most Important Avoidance Measures]
Liustrates the generic measures and possibilities to avoid any of the above mentioned
loss in connectivity:
• Physically separate and redundant WAN-connections from the ITSC to the
network of the WAN provider by means of:
- Redundant and separate link infrastructure (cables, trunks, ducts) to
different PoP (point of presence) of providers network
- Redundant routing/switching components
- Connected to redundant LAN backbone infrastructure
- Redundant firewall and intrusion detection architecture (no single point of
failure)
- Uninterruptible and redundant power supply and fault tolerant air
conditioning
• Permanent (7x24) monitoring within the network management systems and
alerting possibilities (pager, SMS, mobile phone)
• Review and analysis of the providers network and connectivity infrastructure
(within the data path in the end-to-end scenario) regarding:
- Technology (state-of-the-art and life cycle management)
- Support and maintenance processes (also review event logs from other
customers)
- Redundancy
- Availability (failover test)
- Disaster avoidance and recovery measures
- Contract, business stability, changes in market positioning
- Service level, liability and price
To avoid possible outages of WAN implement the following avoidance measure
according to good practice:
• Select and change to a “reliable service” provider to get the requirements
guaranteed
• Always consider the full end to end connectivity
• Establish a 2nd vendor sourcing strategy for the WAN using 2 different
providers for redundancy and fail over
• Invest into 3rd option for dialup networking via ISDN and/or satellite network
• Secure up-to-date spare material (not only routers and switches, but also
cables, plugs and software)
• Ensure a good, structured cabling also for retracing in terms of good practice
• Establish remote support access possibilities

metadata.hierarchy: 3. CRISIS SCENARIO > 3.2 Datacenter MPLS link/ Leased Line Down - Outage of WAN/ Leased line > 3.2.1 Most Important Avoidance Measures

content:
Context: 3. CRISIS SCENARIO > 3.2 Datacenter MPLS link/ Leased Line Down - Outage of WAN/ Leased line > 3.2.2 Most Important Recovery Measures
Content: [3.2.2 Most Important Recovery Measures]
• Establish a frame agreement with a backup data link provider, if not
implementing a fully redundant WAN based on a different (second) WAN
provider. Implement backup of VPN Site to Site.
• Clearly define response, intervention and repair-time with the WAN-provider
within professional SLAs and keep internal resources maintaining the local
part of WAN in line with those specifications
• Use alternative dialup connections to temporary re-establish connectivity to
the sites (e.g. with lower capacity and therefore limited application access)
• Perform regularly recovery tests together with the WAN-provider

Context: 3. CRISIS SCENARIO > 3.3 Core Switch error - Outage of Backbone LAN
Content: [3.3 Core Switch error - Outage of Backbone LAN]
Outage of the Backbone-LAN is characterized by any of the following misbehaviors
within the connectivity or data communication of servers within the data center itself:
• One, several or all Servers are no longer reachable from the outer world
(external to the data center of the ITSC)
• One, several or all servers are disconnected from the WAN, the ITSC LAN
• One, several or all servers are disconnected from each other
• Monitoring of the systems and applications platform is no longer possible
• Applications are not reachable
• Transactions are not processable, if not aborted
• Data in the various systems might be inconsistent or corrupt
• Failover of intended redundancy technology is not working
• Interruption by mis-configuration of malicious change of the environment
All above-mentioned interruptions might be mainly caused by (see risk categorization in
Appendix M) damage to the LAN-BB components by:
• External disaster like fire, thunderstorm, earthquake, flood and other
hazardous incidents
• Human misbehavior (mistakes on changes, sabotage) including
unauthorized access to network components
• Risks originated by capacity overload or system failures
• Organizational risks
Data center risks including loss of power and failure in air conditioning
This Scenariro is define with detail in APPENDIX 1 - Core switch: down

Context: 3. CRISIS SCENARIO > 3.4 Outage of Major Application (SAP/SUN/WINHAM/FILES/SAS System)
Content: [3.4 Outage of Major Application (SAP/SUN/WINHAM/FILES/SAS System)]
Outage of Major Application is characterized by any of the following misbehaviors within
the end-to-end processing of any Major Application user of the PHV:
• Many users are no longer able to use the application environment and the
business critical transactions
• Loss of connectivity or interruption of one, more or all store or with customer
or external partners
• Technology failure or general trouble within the application systems
environment
• Fail over of intended redundancy technology not working
• Interruption by mis-configuration of malicious change of the environment
• Problems on the various interfaces between the system components in the
whole end to end communication path
• Logical interruption or malfunction of system or application functions
All above-mentioned interruptions might be mainly caused by:
• External disaster like fire, thunderstorm, earthquake, flood and other
hazardous incidents
• Human misbehavior (mistakes on changes, sabotage) including
unauthorized access to the application systems
• Organizational risks
• Data center risks including loss of power and failure in air conditioning
• Software bug
In all cases, the consequence will be the interruption of the access to the
application system and its business critical transactions, due to failure in the entire
application environment provided by the ITSC, resulting in the discontinuation of
business processes.
This Scenariro is define with detail in APPENDIX 1 – Application Down

Context: 4. Application Error July 2018 All departments > 4.2. BCP Drills Procedures
Content: [4.2. BCP Drills Procedures]
Prior to the drill
a. The CCT is formed and leader appointed
b. Scenario and timeline for the drill is agreed by the risk management committee or the
CCT

[DRP Technical Table]:
Scenarios* | Planned Drills to be conducted on | Involved departments | Drill Co-ordinator
1. RSC is not accessible | Based on BCP Plan | All departments in the RSC | Financial Controller
2. All server at PHV DC Down | January 2018 | IT departments in the RSC | All departments
3. One host esxi at DC is down ( turned on h.a at dc) | May 2018 | All departments in the RSC and selected stores | Financial Controller
4. Application Error | July 2018 | IT departments in the RSC | All departments


[DRP Technical Table]:
Planned Drills to be
conducted on


[DRP Technical Table]:
Involved
departments

c. Communication is made to the involved personnel regarding the time of the drill
activation
d. CCT to ensure that the involved personnel have access to the most updated BCP
Manual and contact list
e. A team of observers is established to observe and document findings noted during the
drill in a drill event log. Depending on the purpose of management, the team of
observers can be disclosed to the personnel involved in the drill (for prepared drill) or
kept confidential (for surprise drill)
f. On the date when the drill is schedule, the CCT leader initiates the communication via
the communication tree to activate the drill
During the drill
a. The drill is activated and the involved personnel follow the procedures stipulated in the
respective crisis scenario mentioned in the appendix 1
b. The CCT to announce the BCP level ( 3 level of the Crisis) to the involved personnel
c. During the drill, the team of observers to document the findings noted during the drill in a
drill event log
d. The CCT to announce the closure of the drill upon completion
Post the drill
a. The event log is completed and circulated to the CCT members
b. A post-drill evaluation meeting is called to address issues noted in the event log, discuss
and agree on the action plan and expected completion timeline. Learning is
documented for future reference

Context: 1. HỆ THỐNG KẾT NỐI MẠNG DC – DR SITE > 1.2 Mô tả hoạt động
Content: [1.2 Mô tả hoạt động]
- Trong điều kiện làm việc bình thường, hệ thống máy chủ VMware ESX và kết nối
mạng WAN, Internet tập trung về tại Data Center – Văn Phòng chính Pizza Hut
Việt Nam.
- Các cửa hàng kết nối đến tài nguyên mạng đặt tại các server thông qua kết nối
chính MPLS L3 hoặc kết nối dự phòng IPSec VPN qua Internet router.
- Người dùng làm việc ở bên ngoài văn phòng kết nối đến DC thông qua Remote
Acces SSL VPN do Sophos firewall cung cấp.
- DC luôn có 1 kết nối VPN đến hệ thống Production, UAT đặt tại MS Azzue Cloud.
- Người dùng PHV (mạng dây và wifi nội bộ) tại DC sử dụng Internet trên đường
Leased Line (183.91.23.194).
- Người dùng mạng wifi cho khách tại DC sử dụng Internet trên 1 đường riêng
FTTH đặt tại modem Vigor 2900 series.
- Hệ thống trang bị 1 đường MPLS Layer 2 kết nối DC – DR site để phục vụ đồng
bộ cho hệ thống Veam Backup, VMware ESX. Đường này cũng là đường
backup khi đường MPLS Layer3 mất kết nối.
- Tại mỗi thời điểm chỉ có 1 đường MPLS 3 tại DC hoặc DR site làm việc. Đơn vị
cung cấp đường truyền cấu hình thiết bị có thể phát hiện đường truyền có sự cố
và tự động điểu chỉnh route sang site còn lại. Tương tự cho đường Internet
Leased Line.
